import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    ops = []
    
    def rev(p):
        ops.append(p)
        a[:p] = a[:p][::-1]
    
    def bring_to_front(val, limit):
        pos = a.index(val)
        if pos == 0:
            return
        if pos % 2 == 0:  # odd 1-indexed
            rev(pos + 1)
            # now val is at position pos (0-indexed from end of reversed)
            # val was at pos, now at pos+1-1-pos = 0
            # wait: reversing 0..pos, val at index pos goes to index 0
            return
        else:  # even 1-indexed, odd 0-indexed
            rev(pos)  # pos is odd length
            # val at 0-indexed pos, reverse 0..pos-1, val not moved
            # Hmm
            rev(1)  # noop
            rev(pos + 2) if pos + 2 <= limit else None
    
    # Let me just do a simpler brute force for small n
    # Actually let me think again and implement properly
    
    m = n
    while m > 1:
        # place a[m-1] = m
        target = m
        pos = a.index(target)
        if pos != m - 1:
            if pos != 0:
                if pos % 2 == 1:  # odd 0-index = even 1-index
                    rev(pos)
                    pos = 0
                else:
                    rev(pos + 1)
                    pos = 0
            rev(m)
        m -= 1
        if m <= 1:
            break
        target2 = m
        pos2 = a.index(target2)
        if pos2 != m - 1:
            if pos2 != 0:
                if pos2 % 2 == 1:
                    rev(pos2)
                else:
                    rev(pos2 + 1)
            if a[0] != target2:
                pass
            rev(m)
        m -= 1
    
    if a == sorted(a):
        print(len(ops))
        print(*ops)
    else:
        print(-1)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    ops = []
    
    def rev(p):
        ops.append(p)
        a[:p] = a[:p][::-1]
    
    def bring_to_front(val, limit):
        pos = a.index(val)
        if pos == 0:
            return
        if pos % 2 == 0:  # odd 1-indexed
            rev(pos + 1)
            # now val is at position pos (0-indexed from end of reversed)
            # val was at pos, now at pos+1-1-pos = 0
            # wait: reversing 0..pos, val at index pos goes to index 0
            return
        else:  # even 1-indexed, odd 0-indexed
            rev(pos)  # pos is odd length
            # val at 0-indexed pos, reverse 0..pos-1, val not moved
            # Hmm
            rev(1)  # noop
            rev(pos + 2) if pos + 2 <= limit else None
    
    # Let me just do a simpler brute force for small n
    # Actually let me think again and implement properly
    
    m = n
    while m > 1:
        # place a[m-1] = m
        target = m
        pos = a.index(target)
        if pos != m - 1:
            if pos != 0:
                if pos % 2 == 1:  # odd 0-index = even 1-index
                    rev(pos)
                    pos = 0
                else:
                    rev(pos + 1)
                    pos = 0
            rev(m)
        m -= 1
        if m <= 1:
            break
        target2 = m
        pos2 = a.index(target2)
        if pos2 != m - 1:
            if pos2 != 0:
                if pos2 % 2 == 1:
                    rev(pos2)
                else:
                    rev(pos2 + 1)
            if a[0] != target2:
                pass
            rev(m)
        m -= 1
    
    if a == sorted(a):
        print(len(ops))
        print(*ops)
    else:
        print(-1)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
